articles.title: Adrielle's Watch
articles.content:
Adrielle's Watch is an informal, vigilante group that looks after the @[Outlands](settlement:d933131a-640d-4513-b6aa-d99247dfa47a). Most of their time is spent in @[Erinhir](settlement:96d2680b-e669-4c07-ba6b-f4727fb8d9d9) (50%) or @[The Cauldron](settlement:983c8fa2-3df5-46b0-91fb-3dbe004450da) (40%) given that the @[Haven City Guard](organization:d24af512-917c-4a1b-98d0-0b9d9934be00) has stations in @[The Dell](settlement:e8aa9d8f-68be-4d89-9e11-6ba288e71515) and @[The Dwarrow](settlement:fa91fd8a-f74e-409d-a703-26a9b8e09954).

[subcontainer:[var:private]]
Their leader is @[Culvhen](person:10086a16-b266-4c3a-9615-628c2528d3cf), a golden-eyed elf who has developed an excessive fondness for brandy. He keeps in surreptitious contact with Adrielle concerning payment for his watchers' work. There rest of the group is made up of 10 humans, elves, and halflings (see at right) who work for @[Culvhen](person:10086a16-b266-4c3a-9615-628c2528d3cf). All of them do it for the money and care little what happens to the people they spot and capture.

There is a chance that a party will be under the scrutiny of Adrielle's Watch and if so, there is an 8% chance (1 in 12) that some character in the players' party will apparently fit the description of one of those being sought by the Watch. Should this happen, the party will be tailed (the average perceptiveness ratings for the Watch members is 13), and there will be a kidnapping attempt made if the Watch outnumbers the party by 3 to 2 or better, whenever the party reaches an unfrequented area.
[/subcontainer]

[h3]Relationship with @[Haven City Guard](organization:d24af512-917c-4a1b-98d0-0b9d9934be00)[/h3]
The realtionship is one of tolerance on both sides and @[Culvhen's](person:10086a16-b266-4c3a-9615-628c2528d3cf) men will turn over apprehended criminals to the City Guard if any are nearby (40% chance).  In some cases the "criminal" may be dropped off (a little worse for wear) at the nearest Guard Station. Otherwise, they will take things into their own hands.

articles.title: Angelina's Guild
articles.content:
@[Angelina's](person:b297fe62-c085-4612-a439-fc80b5da899d) Guild controls Black Hand interests in smuggling, both by sea and by caravan, and the supply of drugs to many of Haven's noxious opium dens. Auxiliary concerns include the brothel, @[The Black Whip](landmark:6f66e600-9089-49a6-b9dc-7e4dd9febe5c) (DU), on @[The Street of Silk Veils](settlement:587a7a3a-ba45-4bfd-94bc-fe6e17ae023c), and the small but lucrative underground slave trade (a highly illegal business centered around the shop of @[Adrielle Ilorim](person:5d239bee-e2a5-4952-b7ad-412596ca7c50) in Erinhir). Its headquarters are a posh and seemingly respectable dining establishment in @[The North Corridor](settlement:4554e4a9-db69-47da-9921-6c5850001ce8) known as @[Silvante's](landmark:5799fe2e-05a4-44fa-91b3-d10868b94923) owned and operated by the guild.

@[Angelina](person:b297fe62-c085-4612-a439-fc80b5da899d) has four lieutenants who direct various parts of her operation. The first of these is @[G. Maximus](person:ce883af8-d9d1-4c65-bedf-e1442af7bca7),  the huge, obese, pig-eyed owner of @[The Black Whip](landmark:6f66e600-9089-49a6-b9dc-7e4dd9febe5c), a house of pleasure on @[The Street of Silk Veils](settlement:587a7a3a-ba45-4bfd-94bc-fe6e17ae023c) which caters to those who enjoy any of a variety of strange and bizarre fetishes. Maximus lives at his somewhat disreputable establishment, and controls three cells containing some dozen men. One group consists of the three or four thugs in his employ, another of the uniquely trained staff of the Whip, and the third a group of four men who handle Black Hand involvement in the Haven slave trade - the leader of these men, Angus McFarland, is @[Adrielle Ilorim](person:5d239bee-e2a5-4952-b7ad-412596ca7c50)'s contact in @[Erinhir](settlement:96d2680b-e669-4c07-ba6b-f4727fb8d9d9). Maximus himself is as venal and loathsome as the activities he directs, and is well satisfied with his grimy little niche in @[The Black Hand](organization:d1a96263-4191-482e-ace6-360d25106f60) power structure. There are few things beneath him, and it is rumored that he partakes liberally of the services offered by his establishment.

The head of @[Angelina](person:b297fe62-c085-4612-a439-fc80b5da899d)'s drug dealing operation, @[Julian de Carbot](person:547d4772-53c9-48c4-bfdc-045535575bfe), works out of @[Black Padre's](landmark:26f2ee27-6aa5-4573-9aff-51019b20dfd6) (AY), the seedy dockside bar, opium den, and whorehouse located in @[The Commons](settlement:8ff1c19a-c29d-4050-9c7c-7200f23453be). Carbot is short and fat (although he does not begin to approach the massive girth of Maximus), but he is also cunning, devious, and ruthless. He directs the movements of some ten men (three cells), and the drugs that they handle. Padre's serves as one of the principal store places for Black Hand opium before it is sold, along with a rented storage room at Fellini's boarding house, deep in @[The Commons](settlement:8ff1c19a-c29d-4050-9c7c-7200f23453be), and the back room of Andre de Montville's spice emporium in @[The South Docks](settlement:e2e38a16-2c10-443d-b40a-6789aa7c041a), where contraband opium is stored in teakwood boxes marked as cinnamon.

@[Mordred de Laterre](person:cd862acf-c62c-4768-84c7-fea8d1aad3cd), the crippled, wizened, and now retired caravan master who operates a caravanserai in @[The North Corridor](settlement:4554e4a9-db69-47da-9921-6c5850001ce8), is another of @[Angelina's](person:b297fe62-c085-4612-a439-fc80b5da899d) top lieutenants. From his establishment caravans set out for most of the cities along the great road, and often carry Black Hand contraband among their cargo. Mordred does not have many operatives directly under him, for the success of his business is dependent upon legitimate contacts with area caravan masters, and not upon brute force or strength of numbers. Nonetheless, two or three of his employees at the caravanserai are affiliated with the Hand. Since Mordred needs to coordinate the activities of Black Hand agents in the other great cities who act as his buyers and shippers, he also employs several couriers who are constantly moving in and out of Haven with major caravans carrying letters between Mordred and his agents.

By far the most extensive part of this guild is @[The South Docks](settlement:e2e38a16-2c10-443d-b40a-6789aa7c041a) smuggling operation, run by @[Alfonso Caradallo](person:84e38ba5-9ee4-451f-9948-61147035f523). Alfonso, along with three other subordinates of slightly greater brawn but vastly inferior wit, directs a small longshoreman's firm and storage warehouse, known respectively as Longshoremen Inc. and 
@[Caradello's Storage](landmark:061fa499-04c8-404a-a4a2-dd450f701b04). He also supervises the activities of @[Constantino Maylino](person:1d55f105-0e13-485c-956f-ac2c1696e432), owner of a dockside warehouse, Maylino's night watchman @[Rufus](person:16e2a95b-ef60-477f-99ef-237cef2eef14), and @[Lazar Symbocca](person:2c51d376-29e2-4868-ac54-050e58f8c59c), owner of @[Sym's Storage Company](landmark:950bc2b9-c121-4be6-a597-f3373ed455b0). Under the direction of Caradello, Maylino oversees the buying and storage of illicit cargos for the small fleet of Black Hand smugglers that operates along the river. Unknown to Maylino, cargos smuggled into Haven are stored in Caradello's warehouse, or at Sym's Storage.

The final immediate underling of Caradello's is @[Conrad Bissell](person:b32a52d6-39b4-45cd-99b9-14c813a671d3), a small ferret faced man, who is one of the three day-shift assistant customs inspectors employed by the ministry of Ports and Shipping to collect customs duties along @[The South Docks](settlement:e2e38a16-2c10-443d-b40a-6789aa7c041a), under the direction of Inspector @[Valyri Pipper](person:652a7861-c505-4742-85a0-2d8163986283). Caradello attempts to arrange for Black Hand cargos to be inspected by Bissell who places the proper seals and stamps upon the appropriate items. Nonetheless, there is only a 65% chance that Bissell will be the inspector on duty at any given time; illegal Black Hand cargos, therefore, are usually well concealed, even when Caradello has arranged for Bissell to inspect the smuggler. In exchange for his duplicity, Caradello pays Bissell an annual gratuity, and arranged for Bissell's sister to receive training and employment as a courtesan from @[The Black Whip](landmark:6f66e600-9089-49a6-b9dc-7e4dd9febe5c) (small government officials, like beggars, cannot afford to be choosers).

The smuggling vessels themselves are owned and controlled by a variety of means and companies. Two long distance haulers, owned by the Tandouray Spice Company, make port but three or four times per year, and are used to smuggle small, easily concealable, and highly valuable items (like packets of rare spice into Haven, or battle magic out). Five smaller ships belong to the Bilotto Leather Company, a large tannery based in @[The Commons](settlement:8ff1c19a-c29d-4050-9c7c-7200f23453be), owned indirectly by @[Prospero Maggia](person:eb5339e3-7f79-4754-9d9c-85ed69a2c39b). Their traffic is mostly restricted to the river trade, carrying hides from the great downriver Ohlfaardt cattle herds, and they generally make port two or three times per month. However, once or twice a year, as occasion demands, large shipments of opium and hashish from the far Kingdom of Jez are brought into the Dorian Delta by the large Tandouray merchantmen, and unloaded onto the smaller Bilotto river craft in relative privacy over a period of several weeks. Concealed within the bundles of leather, small amounts of drugs are easily carried into Haven, thus avoiding costly taxes (although the use and sale of drugs is legal in Haven, drugs are subject to heavy imposts when brought into the city).

Besides smuggling drugs and spices into Haven, two other items that Caradello handles are spidersilk and foreign wines and liquors. Each is in direct competition with a major Haven industry, and as a consequence are @[Imported](person:85c3b7a4-cbd6-48b9-9b32-6f7bdc14621b) in defiance of the official monopolies granted to powerful members of the Council of Guilders by the Duke of Haven. The wines and liquors are smuggled into the city aboard Falling Star, a large merchantman owned by Culvarran Ltd., whose chief cargos include olive oil from the equatorial city of Karen Te, and molasses from the western city of Belakor; the wine bottles are carried concealed within the larger kegs of oil or syrup. Two other ocean going ships, owned by the weaving firm of brothers Mazov and Kary Toistol, normally import fine wool from the Clermont sheep flocks, out of the small port town of Clermont, fifty miles east of Haven; every two or three months, however, the vessels rendezvous with yet another Hand affiliated ship, the privately owned Dark of the Moon, and receive a consignment of spidersilk, which can then be concealed within heavy bundles of wool for the short voyage to Haven.

Caradello coordinates the movements of his smuggler captains from @[Black Padre's](landmark:26f2ee27-6aa5-4573-9aff-51019b20dfd6); there, Caradello plans the next shipments and disburses the necessary capital to pay for various out-port cargos. Since only a few of his captains are likely to be in port at the same time, Caradello often gives them letters to be delivered to men not present, or to be placed in Hand maintained "letter boxes" in other ports, instructing those men in current plans; @[Esteban de Martin](person:c55c4730-09b5-4fd5-903e-e9d3e652410d), captain of Moon, is the man that Caradello uses most often as a courier.

@[Angelina](person:b297fe62-c085-4612-a439-fc80b5da899d) meets with her lieutenants intermittently to plan large operations, and to receive progress reports on operations already in motion. Most such meetings take place at @[Silvante's](landmark:5799fe2e-05a4-44fa-91b3-d10868b94923), which provides an excellent cover for @[Angelina](person:b297fe62-c085-4612-a439-fc80b5da899d). Other than Caradello, Carbot, Maximus, and de Laterre, only @[Angelina](person:b297fe62-c085-4612-a439-fc80b5da899d)'s headquarters staff (a body guard, a clerk, and two assassins) know the location of @[Angelina](person:b297fe62-c085-4612-a439-fc80b5da899d)'s headquarters; the only others who know @[Angelina](person:b297fe62-c085-4612-a439-fc80b5da899d)'s true identity are her fellow Black Hand guild masters.

articles.title: Ariosto’s Guild
articles.content:
@[Corwin Ariosto](person:c6c20de1-501d-4cb2-9004-132dd41b4bc9) is master of one of @[The Black Hand's](organization:d1a96263-4191-482e-ace6-360d25106f60) two traditional thieving guilds. He is an older, more cautious man, who has been with the Hand for many years. Because of his experience and his known trustworthiness, Ariosto has been assigned the much more difficult territory of @[The South Corridor](settlement:d684e9e8-9b69-45fc-8352-3de0e6e7f870) and adjoining areas including @[The Labyrinth](settlement:e05f9a94-a44d-4991-b419-28cae89db0ee). His guild is larger, containing four cells (almost twenty men) and includes a cell made up of the finest Hand thieves - possibly the best thieving team in Haven, although a few individuals surpass them in skill. 

Ariosto's guild also maintains contact with the ten to fifteen Black Hand thieves who have managed to infiltrate the Thieves Guild, and forwards information gained from them to other Black Hand units when necessary. 

Both guilds emphasize second story operations and team work, and look down upon pickpocketing and individual moonlighting. Because the Hand was originally an operation made up solely of thieves, the two Hand thieves' guilds are prestigious, and much of the Hand leadership for all guilds is drawn from them.

articles.title: Armorers Guild
articles.content:
If you wear or use to fight, it was probably crafted by a member of this guild. Without a meeting hall of their own, they meet in @[The Hall of All Guilds](landmark:b0995c90-f747-40b6-994b-c052b8b4f278).

articles.title: Blackbourne Family
articles.content:
The Blackbourne family first made its fortune through the importation of fine fabrics but have since expanded their sights, and are involved in all aspects of the clothing trade (dyeing, tailoring, and even retail sales). The Blackbournes own several fine clothing shops, and three large sailing vessels. 

The rapid growth of the Blackbourne business interests is due to foresight of its current patriarch, Hamlin, an incredibly vigorous man of sixty who has built up an extensive trade network that brings him the latest fashion concepts from abroad, and has the good sense to commission the best clothiers in the city to produce these innovative new designs. Hamlin’s election as master of the @[Guild of the Loom](organization:d1142744-b028-4d6a-9e23-dce83dacbdf1) was virtually unanimous, and this Guild is not characterized by the petty internal politics that dominate the activities of some of the other professional Brotherhoods. 

Hamlin was recently widowed, and has three adult sons: Anson, Marcus, and Gladiolus. Only Anson is married, to Talia Corio, whose Uncle is master of Haven's Stonemasons Guild; they have one young daughter, Amathea.

The family's home in Haven is located in The @[Heights](settlement:1f0571c0-b8de-4b50-b010-5529cab6603b)

articles.title: Brotherhood of Free Companies
articles.content:
Represents mercenaries and @[Mercenary Companies](organization:1f566952-526a-46ee-924e-c5f90b98505b) present in Haven, including all female companies. Individual female mercenaries gravitate toward the @[Lydian Sisterhood](organization:36371d00-e1ea-4675-93a6-164b28521cad).

articles.title: Brotherhood of the Amalgam
articles.content:
Apothecaries, alchemists, potion makers etc. Anyone who combines ingredients in order to cause a specific effect. This can be to cure, heal, transmute, posion, etc.

Without a guildhall of their own, they meet in @[The Hall of All Guilds](landmark:b0995c90-f747-40b6-994b-c052b8b4f278).

articles.title: Brotherhood of the Broken Blade
articles.content:
The Brotherhood are a group of older, experienced dwarven warriors, who hold in their possession the shards of the legendary Singing Sword of Truth, Waarhite. They seek the dwarven man or woman fated to make the weapon whole again, and to free the ancient dwarven stronghold Arkendelve from its domination by a stalker from the netherworld (see  @[Scenarios](article:088e33cf-5a28-4c3f-bbe5-a243e610d2ff): The Broken Blade). 

The Brotherhood may be encountered all together, at their semi-regular meetings at @[The Stone Hammer](landmark:bc6496c4-1ba0-4a37-9992-4e15a3f097a8) inn, or its individual members may be found at their various places of business. However, if a dwarven player is an experienced fighter and makes more than one appearance in @[The Dwarrow](settlement:fa91fd8a-f74e-409d-a703-26a9b8e09954), he will probably not have to seek them out, for the brothers are likely to be already searching for him.

The details of Waarhite's legacy, and the possible expedition to Arkendelve, are presented in the Scenario description on @[The Broken Blade](plot:4db0d0e3-3179-4e90-855f-ad252b32b4c5); thumbnail profiles of major figures of the Brotherhood are provided below, to guide the GM in handling initial encounters.

Their leader is @[Coppin Oxblood](person:f2187472-08b8-4ed9-869b-f35398447c96), with @[Tolbin Ironhand](person:acd5b995-2b92-47d3-b166-a8ba4c7c48d5) as second.

articles.title: Cariadoc's Guild
articles.content:
@[Cariadoc](person:3961ff81-222f-4834-a0d3-d38621521f3a) is a short, broad-shouldered man with a handsome' face and a wide grin. His cheerful expression conceals a temperament cold and vicious, and a disposition rarely better than dourly melancholy. @[Cariadoc](person:3961ff81-222f-4834-a0d3-d38621521f3a) grins widest while watching the death throes of his enemies, and likes to be present when his men torture and interrogate opponents. He is the master of @[The Black Hand](organization:d1a96263-4191-482e-ace6-360d25106f60) guild that controls assassination and murder for hire, and his thugs and toughs provide protection and strong-arm support for other Hand guilds.

@[Cariadoc's](person:3961ff81-222f-4834-a0d3-d38621521f3a) operation is tripartite. The most elite and highly trained members of his guild are the two small cells of assassins, who are used to eliminate prominent Black Hand enemies, double agents, and others who cross the Hand, or get in its way. The efficiency of these killers, at least for those who know of them, causes them to be one of the Hand's most feared instruments. If the Hand is approached by an outside source to hire contract killers, and the price is right (as much as 50,000 gold pieces if the victim is highly placed or well-guarded), @[Cariadoc's](person:3961ff81-222f-4834-a0d3-d38621521f3a) assassins may be placed upon the job. 

By far the largest part of @[Cariadoc's](person:3961ff81-222f-4834-a0d3-d38621521f3a) guild, however, is formed of the half-dozen cells of thugs in his employ. These thirty-odd men are the core of the Hand's "muscle", and spend much of their time policing shops and businesses under Black Hand protection. At times, they may operate in conjunction with other Hand guilds as required, or perform actions on their own, such as trashing the shops of those recalcitrants who refuse to pay protection, at the request of other Black Hand leaders. 

The third division of @[Cariadoc's](person:3961ff81-222f-4834-a0d3-d38621521f3a) guild is the small cell of professional torturers that he maintains at his headquarters, @[Redonso's Grain Mill](landmark:265108e3-fdb2-40b2-a9a5-47a3d3b14c36) (owned by @[Prospero Maggia](person:eb5339e3-7f79-4754-9d9c-85ed69a2c39b)). Part of the basement of the mill is a well-stocked torture chamber, fitted with the latest and nastiest torture devices. The two torturers operate the mill, whose constant mechanical grinding nicely obscures the screams emanating from the basement when the two are involved with their professional occupation.

articles.title: Circle Chamber
articles.content: Ruling body of the Mage's Guild

articles.title: Citadel Guard
articles.content:
This brightly garbed, and well-armored unit is the only formal military unit in the City. While technically a regiment, their strength is more that of a platoon with about 20 troops. The Guard is the historical protectors of the sanctity of @[Trade Island](settlement:eec2fc60-5565-40fa-9047-69924589c400) from a time when the region was a wilderness backwater in the former empire. 

In addition to guarding @[The Citadel](landmark:7b465292-ac23-4b4d-a13a-92af694b058b), they also patrol Trade Island and act as a local Watch. And though they are funded by the city, the Guard is not part of the @[Haven City Watch](organization:d24af512-917c-4a1b-98d0-0b9d9934be00).

articles.title: Companions of Silverthorne
articles.content: @[Silverthorne](person:6314ff3e-fadd-42ba-aa6c-bf26fc2767bc), aka Prince Celebdil, has a small band of loyal followers, who have sworn to defend him and his interests, and to help him regain his throne.

articles.title: Conindir Family
articles.content:
The Conindir are relatively new to Haven, having resided in the city for only 40 years. The family numbers seven, the patriarch (an outstanding architect), @[Teleril](person:bc2a44c4-5c6e-4d5e-ab5f-46c2d2791193), his wife @[Khelekien](person:65d84133-9176-4cfe-9b19-bc0c6f5a9235),  two sons @[Mirthule](person:57550327-6e3b-461f-8d5f-37f0d75ba55e) and @[Celeblinde](person:41f15dd8-e212-4061-8e40-e045a31d023e), and three young daughters, @[Tehtaavilya](person:8504a82e-d1bb-434a-b12e-2d912acc190a), @[Tariluine](person:58616754-1e2d-4dca-ab4b-7e3dfe593cf4), and @[Finsilivren](person:046e48b3-c811-4c77-b662-9701df18b3dd). Other than @[Teleril](person:bc2a44c4-5c6e-4d5e-ab5f-46c2d2791193), the family is without exceptional note.

[var:private-access-phre3d]The family isn't very powerful in either the political or business structures of the city, since elves do not usually concern themselves with the idiosyncrasies of other races, but they do play a great role in some of the city's major institutions for recreation, construction, and music. They are legitimate equals of the non-elven higher classes, but, beyond their residences in @[Erinhir](settlement:96d2680b-e669-4c07-ba6b-f4727fb8d9d9), they are not considered as nobles in the classical sense. Overall, elves in general and those of @[Erinhir](settlement:96d2680b-e669-4c07-ba6b-f4727fb8d9d9) in particular, are respected more than many of the nobles of the city so they do not force issues with them, after all, what is the point?.
[/subcontainer]

articles.title: Crafters' Guild
articles.content:
The Crafters make things that are useful. This doesn't necesarily mean they aren't interested in form, just that function matters more.

Without a meeting hall of their own, they meet in @[The Hall of All Guilds](landmark:b0995c90-f747-40b6-994b-c052b8b4f278).

articles.title: de Kammeron Family
articles.content:
@[Boccaccio de Kammeron](person:7987c903-5d74-4fd2-ba5a-b45ef9d76f18) and @[Swayze de Kammeron](person:f2d50957-7201-4aa2-aa13-0c6b197730c3) are two young noblemen from the lands to the east of Haven now known as the Free Sector. Having no wealth or lands of their own to serve as a power base, they came to Haven, and used their titles as a bargaining point to obtain financially secure marriages with members of the wealthier guild families (i.e they are nobles-become-merchanter). The de Kammeron's flagrant opportunism has aroused the hatred of some of the established aristocracy, particularly the @[Young Stallions](organization:7340b23d-2c0c-45fd-8222-13b00aa2760c); both are unaffected by such criticisms, and in fact dress in a very elegant, dandified fashion to show off their newly gained riches.

The family's home in Haven is located in The @[Heights](settlement:1f0571c0-b8de-4b50-b010-5529cab6603b)

[var:private-access-phre3d]
[/container]

articles.title: de la Riis Family
articles.content:
The de la Riises are one of the most prominent and long-lived aristocratic households of Haven. Currently, three generations of de la Riises live and work side by side, and are active both as feudal landlords (on their substantial land holdings some 20 miles northeast of the city walls) and as key figures in the city's political conflicts. Count @[Tybalt de la Riis](person:4c0f8c89-3d7b-46e9-8e62-4506ed433370) is the reigning patriarch of the family; though in his seventies, he still plays an active role in family decision making. 

Tybalt has four surviving children living in the area: @[Harz de la Riis](person:35c67bc0-3762-429d-b35e-c4d0eac46490), the eldest son, who has managed the family's land holdings, the county of Brandenwald; Sir @[Bruning de la Riis](person:743beb40-df18-47f5-8bdf-144dcc690b88), a career military man; daughter @[Rutmilla de la Riis](person:cb7ffd37-21dd-4755-bc95-9824a740f20a); and @[Morgan de la Riis](person:2a538a9e-2951-4c43-9f63-ad552ddc25df), a minor officer in the @[Citadel Guard](organization:d8426ad5-1874-4152-82a5-98179c3a4666). All are married and  have children of their own except for Rutmilla. Harz's eldest son, @[Paul de la Riis](person:61db5c85-e436-459e-926e-17ad7e116b03), has already established a growing reputation for himself, as one of the fiery leaders of the @[Young Stallions](organization:7340b23d-2c0c-45fd-8222-13b00aa2760c). 

The family's home in Haven is located in The @[Heights](settlement:1f0571c0-b8de-4b50-b010-5529cab6603b).

[var:private-access-phre3d]There is considerable dissension within this family. Tybalt, Harz, and Paul are continually at one another's throats, arguing over the question of how the de la Riises should use their remaining political influence in the power struggle between the nobility and the Great Guilders. 

A second source of controversy has recently arisen, when Bruning returned to the city after an absence of many years with a new bride, a commoner woman almost half his age named @[K'maill de la Riis](person:8999eb05-e84c-48a7-a490-a49df5429d0d). K'maill has not been accepted by either Rutmilla or by Brunning's daughter from his first marriage, Ingrid; the latter two have spread rumors that K'maill has used gypsy witchcraft to gain a hold over Burning's affections.
[/subcontainer]

articles.title: Disaffected Artisans
articles.content:
A group of individuals one is likely to come across in @[The Dwarrow](settlement:fa91fd8a-f74e-409d-a703-26a9b8e09954), either singly or as a group, is the disaffected artisans seeking to increase their role in the political life of the city. They can frequently be encountered together at @[The Delver's Trove](landmark:b4343e57-979d-4794-8008-073dd9c1bfa3) tavern, or in one another's shops. They are also @[The Dwarrow](settlement:fa91fd8a-f74e-409d-a703-26a9b8e09954) residents most likely to be encountered abroad in Haven, pleading their case with other interest groups unhappy with the current pattern of Guild rule. 

Depending on the player's affiliations, encounters with the artisans will take one of three forms (regardless of the particular artisan encountered):
[ul]
[li]If the player character is a dwarf from a Guild or Merchant background, he will be asked to join the group at their next get-together to discuss their common problem.[/li]
[li]If the player character is known to be affiliated with the Guild leadership, the artisans will levy some sort of verbal attack at him - ranging from cajoling him to switch sides to threatening his continued safety if he does not[/li].
[li]If the player character falls in neither of the above classes, the artisans will try to rally him to their cause, and to convince him to contribute any monetary or political clout he possesses to this struggle.[/li]
[/ul]
What will differ in these encounters, depending on the particular artisans being spoken to, is the actual course of action being proposed, and the degree of risk to which the player-character is expected to subject himself.

If the player encounters @[Duumin](person:8be5a01c-0686-4dfc-b31f-2cf577576491) (Apex Metalworks), @[Borin](person:fa9c27b4-4feb-4a3e-a748-e38380fdf191) the clockmaker, or @[Malakki](person:ab3e61ce-4944-442f-ad53-c29cab4c4cbe) the jewel smith, the artisan's ire will be directed exclusively at the Cellini family, and the support requested will consist of some sort of direct action against the Cellini's (anything from a boycott of his products, or to defeat of one of Salvatore's pet political initiatives, down to downright vandalism or sabotage; the more these dwarves feel they can trust you, the nastier the suggestions get).

If the artisans encountered include @[Konig](person:e6dc3031-c63d-4249-8ccb-6a6d3d61fc97) the Master Armorer, @[Zaubel](person:3efc9906-6742-4907-b590-09a695211e9f) the jeweler, or @[Vassig](person:4a6903a0-9414-4c5d-950b-e802a3ae3c83) the metalworker, their objectives will be to raise support for the return of the aristocracy to power in the city. The player character may be asked for either overt (participation in a rally, signing a petition) or covert (financial contribution, and invitation to a secret planning meeting) support of this cause. If the player-character is himself of noble blood, any NPC encounter initiated by the artisans would almost certainly be made by members of this royalist group.

It is possible that, if a player allows himself to get involved in the more violent schemes of the unhappy artisans, he may be contacted shortly thereafter by @[Tacobur](person:c721435d-0ec1-4fd3-aeb0-6d498aafd814) the artisan or @[Bethli](person:5d5a4b6e-bd15-4afb-8d94-fdfe0f7d6b02) the smelter owner. Both of these wealthy dwarven merchants take a neutral stance in the Guild-Noble conflict, and seek to keep matters peaceful. Both dwarves have well-placed connections around town which make it worth a player's while to disengage himself from such illicit activities, if either feel the contemplated action is important enough to pose a real danger to the city's political stability.

articles.title: Elengul Family
articles.content:
The Elengul have resided in Haven for almost eighteen centuries; at present, only two of the line actually live in the city, the patriarch and ruling head of the family, @[Skorindir Elengul](person:2566914e-1e5d-41d0-94b7-c3d10349fb3c), and his youngest son, @[Taubardur](person:4bf38b25-3671-441b-ad91-c557b6c61c3f). They collaborate in the management of the Loft, one of the finer eating places in the city, and one of the few places in @[Erinhir](settlement:96d2680b-e669-4c07-ba6b-f4727fb8d9d9) in which non-elves are generally welcome.

[var:private-access-phre3d]The family isn't very powerful in either the political or business structures of the city, since elves do not usually concern themselves with the idiosyncrasies of other races, but they do play a great role in some of the city's major institutions for recreation, construction, and music. 

They are legitimate equals of the non-elven higher classes, but, beyond their residences in @[Erinhir](settlement:96d2680b-e669-4c07-ba6b-f4727fb8d9d9), they are not considered as nobles in the classical sense. Overall, elves in general and those of @[Erinhir](settlement:96d2680b-e669-4c07-ba6b-f4727fb8d9d9) in particular, are respected more than many of the nobles of the city  so they do not force issues with them (after all, what is the point?).
[/subcontainer]

articles.title: Free Traders
articles.content:
They are the independent, travelling businessmen who provide a good portion of all intercity trade. Their wares are sold mainly to in-town merchants, but they also retail small quantities of easily transported goods at the local market / bazaar. Free Traders are not organized as a group, but are bound together by customs and traditions as old as the Road. Outside of the normal competition of business, they are usually friendly, and deal fairly with one another, even with those they have never met before. They will readily lend each other help if needed (particularly in catching thieves), and trade among themselves at wholesale rates. Frequently, they travel in groups, for the safety of all and are an excellent source of information on the safety of the roads.

Some traders specialize in a particular line of products, such as spices, potions, or medicines. Many others tend to carry whatever is profitable, such as precious stones from the Thanamyl region. The growing merchant guilds, however, are beginning to monopolize more and more of the trade along major roads and rivers. They travel in larger, well-guarded caravans, bringing in a greater bulk of goods at lower cost. The activities of the guilds are a sore point with many of the Free Traders, and a constant subject of complaint around the nightly campfires. There are those that seek to form another guild of sorts by organizing the Free Traders, but the traders are  a fiercely independent folk, and so far this has not been particularly successful.

articles.title: Geronde Family
articles.content:
House Geronde is a noble family from Haven, originating from the city's founding. The family's motto is “What you fear, we master''. The family crest features a wrecked grinning monster’s face on the yellow background. It was founded by the famous dragon slayer Ekhan Geronde approximately 5 generations ago.   [subcontainer:1aad823c-af35-4a92-b9c1-93444e9706f4]@[Nesan Geronde](person:f5ad9047-5b50-4cba-87d3-65b000969493), Ekhan's grandson, haunts the family mausoleum (see @[Key: Sins of the Past](plot:b5bb373f-d517-444f-8eb0-ab626e3924db)). [/subcontainer]

Generations ago the Geronde Family was known in Haven as fearless and experienced monster hunters who always succeeded in their job. They specialized in the many uses of monsters, they capture and sell monsters both as pets and guard beasts. Those that couldn't be tamed are harvested for every part of their body, from their skin and bones to their flesh and organs. 

[var:private-access-phre3d]Eventually the family fell upon hard times and lost most of its greatness until Lady @[Rowena Geronde](person:c44f3336-7f83-4e89-a5c8-78896cc3f650) became its matriarch. Rowena decided to beat the new merchant class at their own game using her abilities as an enchantress of magical items. She now runs by far the largest establishment producing magically-enhanced goods in Haven. Her shrewd business sense has enabled her to prosper, and she has used a sizable portion of that newfound wealth to repurchase the family's land holdings, and restore their stately mansion in The @[Heights](settlement:1f0571c0-b8de-4b50-b010-5529cab6603b), just inside the city walls. 

Rowena has been widowed for ten years, and is often preoccupied with business matters; consequently the family (now consisting of her husband's deaf-mute brother Udral, Rowena, and her four children) is not a close-knit one, and each of its members lead largely separate lives.
[/subcontainer]

articles.title: Government of Haven
articles.content:
[h1]Governor of Haven[/h1]
The title of governor is hereditary and the current holder is @[Duke Fernando di Cotillion](person:4ead6296-2499-4be8-984e-f8b35b973d6c). While his family was forced to surrender significant day-to-day power 175 years ago, the Duke still holds a great deal of power in the city. The duke is still responsible for major decisions and approves decisions of the @[Council of Boroughs](organization:61d43984-7e7d-4dc1-9b87-2df67775b86d) acting upon their 'advice'. In rare instances the governor can veto a council decision. The governor is also the source of many political appointments and other positions determined by patronage such as the High Judges, who serve for a term of ten years in the Court of Common Pleas (civil law), the Court of the Exchequer (taxation), or the Court of the Eagle Standard (the high ducal court dealing with treason).  

[h1]@[Council of Boroughs](organization:61d43984-7e7d-4dc1-9b87-2df67775b86d)[/h1]
Day-to-day government of the city is in the hands of the Council of Boroughs, a group comprised of officials elected from each of the city's six boroughs plus one representative from the  @[Council of Masters](article:84ab9d0c-57eb-4001-93ea-df86f82721df) (note that @[Trade Island](settlement:eec2fc60-5565-40fa-9047-69924589c400) is administratively part of @[The North Corridor](settlement:4554e4a9-db69-47da-9921-6c5850001ce8) and doesn't have its own representative). Membership in the council for the individual borough depends on the number of people residing in that area. 

Lately, the wealthy merchant families, especially the Grand Guilders, have been attempting to wield more power by throwing their resources behind certain candidates in order to stack the council.  These families, the Blackbournes, the Cellinis, the Maggias, the Varrasches, and the elven family of @[Nimrodel](person:c23b1ec6-6ac7-4c88-bff2-ef81a0a78957) the shipwright, have yet to be successful but their influence is growing.

[h1]The Mayor[/h1]
The Council selects the mayor, who acts as a chief executive, responsible for the actual management of the city, and the Mayor proposes the various Ministers for the Council to approve. The current Mayor, @[Elsbeth Windermere](person:b2466c19-d35e-45af-b070-831261248185), is relatively honest, but she is not a perfect judge of men - one of her appointees is crass, greedy, and venal, a man whose only thoughts appear to be to line his own pockets as expeditiously and thoroughly as possible.

[h1]Haven Ministries[/h1]
The various Ministries are managed by one of the Mayor's appointees and generally functions without recourse to the Council - when something needs doing, it gets done. The Council would like to have a touch more power in these situations, so there is some tension with the Mayor.

[h2]Public Streets[/h2]
tbd

[h2]Highways[/h2]
Minister @[Boldan Hansumrogge](person:70454875-c819-4c20-bcbf-739e1023eb29) is charged with the suppression of banditry  and  routine maintenance of the roadways around Haven to a distance of 5 days travel by horse. He can often be seen galloping into Haven from either the Horse Gate in @[The South Corridor](settlement:d684e9e8-9b69-45fc-8352-3de0e6e7f870), or the Caravan Gate in the North.

[h2]Health and Sanitation[/h2]
The Ministry of Sanitation is responsible for keeping the sewers in workable condition and removal of large amounts of offensive materials among other things. Ministry staff are mostly dwarves, although a number of tieflings have been hired for some of the less desirable positions - it is not unusual to see a dwarf or an tiefling popping out of an open manhole in many of the residential sections.  They have been tagged with the collective nickname of "Stinky" by the citizens of Haven for the obvious reason, however, it is usually cause for a rousing brawl to so address one of them.

[h2]Public Peace[/h2]
This ministry is responsible for the @[Haven City Watch](organization:d24af512-917c-4a1b-98d0-0b9d9934be00) and the militia. They also provide a liaison between the city and the various mercenary bands that call Haven home.

[h2]Ports and Shipping[/h2]
@[Freggo the Hairless](person:21a8ba41-030c-4bab-a38d-369dd14171a7)

articles.title: Haven City Watch
articles.content:
The Haven City Watch is equivalent to a modern police force in actions though they do not fully investigate any but the most serious or notorious crimes. They are charged with keeping the general peace in the city and ensuring the safety of the inhabitants. There are approximately 1,100 men in the Watch, with 13 stations (seven of them at the city's gates), the prison, and the training facility. 

The @[Citadel Guard](organization:d8426ad5-1874-4152-82a5-98179c3a4666), stationed on @[Trade Island](settlement:eec2fc60-5565-40fa-9047-69924589c400), is not considered part of the City Watch, even though the city pays the salaries of the guardsmen stationed there. @[Adrielle's Watch](organization:81808025-b365-4b84-9f1f-9a7025b37278) is definitely not part of the City Watch and is tolerated only because they patrol the @[Outlands](settlement:d933131a-640d-4513-b6aa-d99247dfa47a) where the Watch is thin on the ground.

Approximately one-quarter of the Watch is on duty at any given time. The men tend to work 3 weeks of five 8-hour shifts each, and one week of 6 shifts. Some of the Watch are married, or have semi-permanent arrangements, and maintain separate living quarters. About half the Watch prefers to live at various barracks buildings to which they are assigned.

Halfway between the Judgment Gate and the Horse Gate can be found @[The Academy](landmark:4bdf469a-ba1e-4ef0-80b4-aa67f33878af), where training is provided for the city watchmen once they have joined the force. The acedemy is made up of three main areas: [ul][li]@[Academy Barracks](landmark:7e66dea3-678b-488c-989c-e2f2a2b03d50) (headquarters for attachments south of the river)
[li] @[Academy Armory](landmark:439681aa-c25d-4f28-af69-5ddd709f8547) 
[li] @[Academy Training Field](landmark:c9b1cf0e-472a-491b-bbaf-4f87e5462360)
[/ul]

The combat training given the recruits concentrates on peacekeeping rather than soldierly combat, since their normal duty is within the city walls. However, the thousand men of the Watch are the second closest thing Haven has to a standing army since the @[Ducal Guard](organization:91d818fb-8ac1-46f2-8caf-8579c7cdf77e) in @[The Citadel](landmark:7b465292-ac23-4b4d-a13a-92af694b058b) on @[Trade Island](settlement:eec2fc60-5565-40fa-9047-69924589c400) receives more actual field training in military combat techniques. 

On the grassy training field across a narrow alley from the Barracks, many of Haven's citizenry parade and countermarch for their monthly militia training duty. It is unlikely that these citizen soldiers, armed with spears and clad in light leather armor, could do much against veteran troops, but they can at least man the walls in case of attack. 

Each Watchman has a group of ten men under him, and is responsible for their training one day a month. On this day, he meets them at the field, escorts them (usually in whatever semblance of military formation he can shout and bully them into) to the Armory, arms and armors them. He then takes them back to the field and, as one militia trainee put it, "drills the bluidy buitts off 'em!".  Training lasts from mid-morning until dusk, and is required; citizens failing to show up for their assigned day of training will be hauled before a magistrate and fined with repeat offenders possibly drawing duty as targets for fledgling archers.
[anchor|Ducal_Guard]

[h2]The Ducal Guard[/h2]
The City Watch contingent in @[The Heights](settlement:1f0571c0-b8de-4b50-b010-5529cab6603b) is referred to as the Ducal Guard despite being under the command of the city, not the Duke. The unit name descend from the part when they used to be the protectors of the Duke's household.

The Ducal Guard patrols in irregular, but roughly hourly intervals during daylight hours; roughly half-hourly after sunset. There are 60 men in the contingent, based in a fine, stone block, two-story building near Shield Gate. Most are trained cavalrymen and mounted on blood-bay mares supplied by Varrasch and Family. 

Their leader is @[Mikyl Swiftsword](person:403bf324-8208-4b8f-98d1-8e98ded30ba5), a renowned campaigner and overall Commander of the Watch.

[h2]Ranks[/h2]
[b]Commander of the Watch:[/b] This individual is also the Borough Commander for the @[Heights](settlement:1f0571c0-b8de-4b50-b010-5529cab6603b).  Less formally they are called Watch Commander

[b]Borough Commander:[/b] Responsible for their station and all other stations in their borough; reports to the Commander of the Watch 

[b]Station Captain:[/b] Responsible for a station and its compelement of guard; reports to their Borough Commander

[b]Station Officer:[/b] Reports to and assists the station captain including scheduling partols; acts as an intermediary between the men and senior command; larger stations may have multiple Station Officers

[b]Sergeant of the Watch:[/b] Leads a squad of up to 15 guards;  reports to the Station Officer

[b]Watchman:[/b] basic rank and file, reports to a Sergeant of the Watch

[b]Trainee:[/b] Junior member still undergoing training

[subcontainer:1aad823c-af35-4a92-b9c1-93444e9706f4]
[h2]Joining the Watch[/h2]
The Watch does recruit and looks for men with strength, agility, and leadership capability. The pay is not spectacular but the hours and the work are relatively easy. A trainee makes 7gp a week, a watchman 12gp, a sergeant 20gp, a station officer 30gp, and a station captain 50gp (60gp if he is the overall commander for a borough). A watchman may live at his barracks if he wishes and two meals a day are provided in the barracks mess hall. After a year of service the Watch will permit a one-month leave of absence per year.

If a player-character wishes to join the watch, there are many different possibilities. Joining the watch does require certain physical minimums, a total of 40 points in STR, DEX, CON, and CHA. Recognizing the utility of magic, casters are also welcome with the requisite stats being DEX, CON, INT, CHA.  The sum of the four requisites represents the character's base percentage chance of acceptance - add 5% for each level greater than 1st. A martial class character may also apply for a rank higher than trainee: 
[ul][li]Intermediates may apply to start as watchman without undergoing training. 
[li]Veterans may start as sergeants, roughly patrol leader. 
[li]Elites may start at station officers
[/ul]
When applying for advanced positions, acceptance is strictly on stat point total percentage - no bonus for level. 
 
While involved in normal duties, a Watch member has a 2% chance of becoming engaged in a potentially fatal engagement.
[/subcontainer]
articles.snippet: The Haven City Watch is equivalent to a modern police force in actions though they do not fully investigate any but the most serious or notorious crimes. They are charged with keeping the general peace in the city and ensuring the safety of the inhabitants.

articles.title: Hochritter Family
articles.content:
The Hochritters are of the old noble families of Haven, but their fortune has greatly dissipated over the years, and they have been forced to sell off many of their personal valuables and almost all of their land. They now own only a small piece of acreage surrounding their city estate; the home itself shows substantial signs of decay (leaks in the roof, rotting porch columns, etc.).

The family's home in Haven is located in The @[Heights](settlement:1f0571c0-b8de-4b50-b010-5529cab6603b)

[subcontainer:1aad823c-af35-4a92-b9c1-93444e9706f4]
[/subcontainer]

articles.title: Hofstra Family
articles.content:
Living in @[The Glens](settlement:e11d38ab-9461-4be2-b745-34ddf86ec7fb) neighbourhood in the @[North Corridor](settlement:4554e4a9-db69-47da-9921-6c5850001ce8), the Hofstra clan consists of the patriarch, @[Jaime](person:3c39acd4-67f1-40a3-8ef1-ba378524e0f1), his four sons, @[Abel](person:12a241b5-fcfe-4712-817f-0a12106d70e0), @[Bakka](person:b54d3eaa-536b-4110-a201-39e78fa5c9e9), @[Charlie](person:2a76cfbd-2b71-47b8-b505-c9cad32e55af), and @[Dauge](person:985abb36-bd46-4191-a8d9-aaf0a14bade2), and their wives (who are of little consequence). Jaime's only daughter, @[Eezie Rand](person:d9f19bc9-2b69-4601-ae9d-3db42041272f), is married to the youngest @[Rand](organization:ba23c932-2c9e-4193-91be-8664c1476264) brother, @[Zalle](person:25c3009e-ebca-4784-a006-a813e43739ee), but still keeps in close contact with her family. 

The two older sons assist @[Jaime](person:3c39acd4-67f1-40a3-8ef1-ba378524e0f1) in running the bank, determining investments, foreclosing on overdue loans, etc. @[Charlie](person:2a76cfbd-2b71-47b8-b505-c9cad32e55af), and @[Dauge](person:985abb36-bd46-4191-a8d9-aaf0a14bade2) run the courier service which connects the main branch in Haven with the branches in the rest of the @[Ten Cities](organization:0efc56fc-301e-4d3f-ba8c-019da1b01590) - they are presently engaged in trying to set up a coach and mail line to compete with the successful venture run by Gillaume Fargot and Andre Welshe. 

[var:private-access-phre3d]The family fortune makes the Hofstra one of the five richest families in Haven but show few pretension of power.
[/subcontainer]

articles.title: Ilorim Family
articles.content:
The family Ilorim is also relatively new to Haven.  @[Firlirrel Ilorim](person:b6f4ea90-5699-457a-bbd3-4c56917306cf), who is presently accounted as one of the five finest voices in Haven, arrived only some 120 years ago. After making a great impression on the Entertainers' Guild with his superb voice (as well as on the elves), he wooed, won, and married @[Lachoriel](person:dad2aa8f-106f-4c05-aab6-0c9b7f40b572), a beautiful elfin maid of Londhin, a minor family in @[Erinhir](settlement:96d2680b-e669-4c07-ba6b-f4727fb8d9d9), and produced a daughter, @[Adrielle](person:5d239bee-e2a5-4952-b7ad-412596ca7c50). 

@[Adrielle](person:5d239bee-e2a5-4952-b7ad-412596ca7c50) disappeared for a number of years when still a very young girl of 30, but eventually returned, and now runs a shop (@[Fine Silks and Fabrics](landmark:123c418e-9d2e-4cf9-affe-234e2cb1317d)) on Lorien Street in  @[Erinhir](settlement:96d2680b-e669-4c07-ba6b-f4727fb8d9d9) specializing in silks and other fine fabrics.

@[Firlirrel Ilorim](person:b6f4ea90-5699-457a-bbd3-4c56917306cf) is still a power on @[The Street of Harps](settlement:6053011d-37a6-4d6b-8e0b-41925f3bbd77), but seldom performs now other than on special occasions. He is engaged in writing a history of the elvish peoples, and devotes most of his time to that project.

[var:private-access-phre3d]The family isn't very powerful in either the political or business structures of the city, since elves do not usually concern themselves with the idiosyncrasies of other races, but they do play a great role in some of the city's major institutions for recreation, construction, and music. 

They are legitimate equals of the non-elven higher classes, but, beyond their residences in @[Erinhir](settlement:96d2680b-e669-4c07-ba6b-f4727fb8d9d9), they are not considered as nobles in the classical sense. Overall, elves in general and those of @[Erinhir](settlement:96d2680b-e669-4c07-ba6b-f4727fb8d9d9) in particular, are respected more than many of the nobles of the city, so they do not force issues with them (after all, what is the point?).
[/subcontainer]

articles.title: Legal System
articles.content:
The Duke appoints the High Judges, who serve for a term of ten years in the Court of Common Pleas (dealing with civil law), the Court of the Exchequer (the court which enforces taxation), or the Court of the Eagle Standard (the high ducal court dealing with treason). A further court, the @[Circle Chamber](organization:61e67a7d-70ca-4b03-818c-e3f7beb47566), is formed of the highest ranking mages with ducal sanction to regulate the use of magic in Haven.

The High Judges in turn appoint a number of lower level judges as necessary based on case loads and severity. For ease of reference, most trials invloving player characters will be in front of one of the High Judges.

Trials are conducted without juries. The judge hears the case, and then makes the decision as to guilt or innocence - if the defendant is guilty, he also determines sentence.

[h2]The High Judges[/h2]
The five High Judges sit on the majority of criminal cases in Haven, and on any civil cases of more than minimal notoriety. Each Judge is described with a coding of bribability and greed (bribe rating can easily change with the particular trial, and should be determined for each different case). Likes and dislikes for each judge are in the section on lawyers. 

For additional detail, refer to @[Judges in Haven](article:44ebd487-1f8a-4b6f-a08e-f8d1cff1740f).

[h2]Lawyers, Barristers, and Attorneys[/h2]
The effect of a lawyer on the outcome of a trial is of interest, since it is not unlikely that a player-character might be apprehended in the commission of a crime of some sort. It is usually helpful to have a lawyer representing the character in this situation, particularly if he IS guilty. It is also possible for player-characters to function as lawyers (provided the character has training for the courtroom - magistracy).

For additional detail, refer to @[Lawyers in Haven](article:da3ccb78-bf9b-40ae-8405-bab3fe8ea07a).

[subcontainer:1aad823c-af35-4a92-b9c1-93444e9706f4]
[h2]Conducting the Trial[/h2]
Basically, to determine the outcome of a trial you roll d100, adjust for the various modifiers and look up the results on that judge's decision table. There are however some differences between a criminal and a civil trial. For details, refer to @[Factors Affecting Trials ](article:60be5bba-0e42-41d9-a502-26d5a961751f).

[h3]Criminal trials[/h3]
[quote]
[ol]
	[li]Roll d100 [roll:d100]
  [li]Add lawyer's ability modifier
  [li]Add lawyer / judge relationship modifier
  [li]Add judge bribery modifier
  [li]+15% if the client is actually innocent
  [li]Add any other modifier appropriate (role playing, etc.)
  [li]Look up the resulting total on that judge's decision table.
[/ol]
[/quote]
[br]
This will provide verdict and sentence in one step. Crimes are specified as either minor (petty theft, vagrancy, drunkenness, etc.) or major (murder, rape, grand theft, treason, etc.). Use the line on the judge's table that best fits the type of crime.

[h3]Civil Trial[/h3]
Civil suits are a different story when it comes to trial. In most cases, one can assume that the judge is going to make his decision based on all the factors. 
[quote]
[ol]
	[li]Add lawyer skill and lawyer/judge relationship modifiers for the plaintiff
  [li]Add lawyer skill and lawyer/judge relationship modifiers for the defendant
  [li]Bribery modifiers go to the side paying the largest bribe unless bribes are equal, in which case they cancel out
  [li]+25% for whichever side has "Right on one's side" if that applies
  [li]Add any other modifiers the GM feels are appropriate
  [li]The decision roll Target = Plaintiff total / (Plaintiff total + Defendant total)
  [li]Roll d100 [roll:1d100] and if less than or equal to the target the plaintiff wins
[/ol]
[/quote]
[/subcontainer]

articles.title: Lucca's Guild
articles.content: @[Paraggio Lucca](person:73f7f2e9-fa06-4203-80da-58b179b43e9d), the master of one of @[The Black Hand](organization:d1a96263-4191-482e-ace6-360d25106f60)’s two traditional thieving guilds is a slender, boyish man with dark hair and an angular face. His guild consists of some three cells of highly competent thieves who operate mostly in @[The Commons](settlement:8ff1c19a-c29d-4050-9c7c-7200f23453be) and North Corridor.

articles.title: Lydian Sisterhood
articles.content: The sisterhood was created as a home for female mercenaries who didn't feel welcome in any of the other mercenary guilds.

articles.title: Mage's Guild
articles.content:
Most practitioners of magic in Haven are members of this guild, though it is not as strict on membership as the @[Guild of Entertainers](organization:f4889184-3aed-47bb-b7cb-0beeec445e22). The guild also is reponsible for training through the @[College of the Mystic Arts](landmark:576fa97a-08bd-41d1-b588-f7d903051ea0), which is part of the @[University of Haven](organization:6ff2021c-f034-4831-9af5-51b6f4177316), despite being located away from the main univerity grounds (@[Scholar's Triangle](settlement:b401687a-fa7e-4689-beb0-d0a84e91ce24)).

The guild is led by members of the @[Circle Chamber](organization:61e67a7d-70ca-4b03-818c-e3f7beb47566), whose master is @[Karilinia Inturisdan](person:f4f671ed-3c46-47d1-aa37-4b13ca12d3c9).

Both the guild meetings and @[Circle Chamber](organization:61e67a7d-70ca-4b03-818c-e3f7beb47566) meetings are held at the @[Guildhouse of Magic](landmark:51d33666-774a-483c-8543-aa6f1f13f39e) on @[Magic Street](settlement:7f47361e-705c-471d-9c89-8beaa6aad852).

Note that while alchemy and potions might be considered magic, these practitions are part of the Guild of Apothecaries, Alchemists and Potioners.

articles.title: Maggia Family
articles.content:
The Maggias are an enormous family of merchants, with more than 50 blood relatives located within the city of Haven, and almost 100 other in-laws, cousins, and live-in acquaintances (apprentices, wards, old family friends with no other place to go). [var:private-access-phre3d]This book can only mention a few of these individuals, but it may help the GM to remember that any Maggia home will constantly be filled with a variety of relations and hangers-on, giving him leeway to insert almost any kind of NPC encounter he wishes. [/subcontainer]

The family made its original fortune in the winemaking business. @[Gilberto Maggia](person:f8abcaf4-8f94-42f6-84ea-94c3c8c1b1a4), the family's patriarch, is both head of the @[Guild of Provenders](organization:8880f281-e0e8-4dde-9ff9-a9251f1fbae9) that governs all the food production trades (winemaking, butchering, baking,  brewing, etc.) and a member of the Council of Borough. There has been a Maggia on the Council of Boroughs since it was first established, and their opinion has traditionally carried great weight in the Council's deliberations.

Gilberto and his sisters Portia and Gallia form the nucleus of the branch of the Maggia that live in @[The Heights](settlement:1f0571c0-b8de-4b50-b010-5529cab6603b); they want to reduce rather than intensify the conflict between the nobles and the guildsmen, and seek to identify compromises that can bring the two groups together to work toward a common prosperity. 

The family's home in Haven is located in @[The Heights](settlement:1f0571c0-b8de-4b50-b010-5529cab6603b)

Another faction of the family, led by Gilberto's younger brother Prospero, lives in @[The Commons](settlement:8ff1c19a-c29d-4050-9c7c-7200f23453be) section of Haven, and make their money through heavier industrial enterprises like grain milling. Prospero loudly defames his siblings for abandoning their simple origins, and claims that they have become as pretentious as the nobles they supposedly supplanted as the city's administrators.

[var:private-access-phre3d]Prospero has been frequently linked by rumor to the activities of the underworld organization known as @[The Black Hand](organization:d1a96263-4191-482e-ace6-360d25106f60), though no criminal charge has ever been levied against him (for more information on the operations of @[The Black Hand](organization:d1a96263-4191-482e-ace6-360d25106f60). Gilberto and Prospero have not spoken to each other for many years and communicate with each other solely by innuendo. [/subcontainer]

articles.title: Mercenary Companies
articles.content:
One of the things that helps Haven stay a Free City is the presence of several mercenary companies that have chosen to make Haven their headquarters. These companies range from the 50 men in the cataphract cavalry (very heavily armed and armored) force of Buren Tasidek through the 100 seasoned dwarves of Bonur arn Jasti and the 200 strong centaur contingent of Galidonos Goldenhoof to the small army of 500 commanded by Donelek of Petiz. The companies frequently leave the city on assignments, and may be gone for as long as two years, but always seem to return - a little battered, maybe, and with a few holes in the ranks - but none so far has failed to come back.

Even with a majority of the companies gone at one time or another, there are seldom less than a dozen assorted companies in the city. The soldiers use the city's facilities for rest and recreation, and are free-spending enough to assuage the merchants' bad feelings about the ruckuses and uproars caused by the men's antics (@[Street Encounters](plot:ed9475dd-aa16-4b27-afb4-0f19689d8d6c) [url:https://www.worldanvil.com/w/antheros-phre3d/a/street-encounters-plot#Swaggering_Mercs]Swaggering Mercs[/url] and [url:https://www.worldanvil.com/w/antheros-phre3d/a/street-encounters-plot#Cavalry_Troop]Cavalry Troop[/url]).

Occasionally, in the past, various companies have been hired for "road-cleaning" (bandit disposal) duty along the Long Road. In the aftermath of these efforts, the Road often seems unusually peaceful for long durations, with a large number of well decorated oak trees - the mercenary companies tend to do a relatively thorough job of cleanup.

A number of reasonably large mercenary contingents (200 to 500 men each) regularly station themselves at the three towns of the Borgonnin Pass through the Mountains of Peace, the pass taken by the Long Road. They provide protection for groups of travelers and caravans using the pass, which is unfortunately infested with Lizarim (the townspeople appreciate the mercenaries' presence too - fewer successful raids by the lizard-men). These companies are nearly always on the lookout for new recruits, since the casualty rates for duty in the Pass are not low. The pay is high, about five times the regular rates, but the risk factor is at the same level.

In the city, the sworn swords (the common appellation for mercenaries in general) will tend to congregate at the hall of the major guild, @[Free Companies House](landmark:c7102ee5-78b8-460e-81c5-716629f47e31) (located on Caravan Street near the corner of Galenkris), or at the Lydian House the home in Haven, of the @[Lydian Sisterhood](organization:36371d00-e1ea-4675-93a6-164b28521cad) (other mercenary guilds exist but are not represented in Haven). At other times, many mercenaries can be found at one of three different taverns in the area of the Free Companies guild house, @[The Brass Blade](landmark:03f231b8-34d8-4e3d-8cc3-0a4eaec0136a), @[Ludwig's Brauhaus](landmark:13c7ac61-8c01-44ab-aace-459e5255cdca), and the @[Bull of Anathor](landmark:6a6e75ad-7ca7-47e0-bc79-3e6355bf38da). Few outsiders (obvious non-soldiers) are welcomed at these taverns when the mercenaries are in full attendance, but likely recruits might be tolerated (at least for a beer or two, and a question or three).

[h2]Signing Up[/h2]
Joining a mercenary company is not overly difficult. Surviving in one may be. Most companies are in need of fighting personnel at any given time, and will accept anyone who is relatively strong, dexterous, and trained with the weapons favored by the men of the company - swords, pikes, etc. To determine whether a character would be acceptable to a company commander as a recruit: 
[br][br]
[ul]
  [li]Total STR, DEX and CON 
  [li]Add 2 for each point of STR > 14, DEX > 13, and/or CON > 15. 
  [li]Add 5 if the potential recruit is Intermediate, 15 if Veteran, and 20 if Elite
  [li]Add 5 for every weapon favored by the company in which the character is trained, 10 additional for each of these in which he has expertise/proficiency 
  [li]If the company uses shields, add 10 for shield training
  [li]Add 2 for each combat plus with proper weapons
  [li]If the recruit can provide their own mount, add 10
[/ul]

Using the total as a percentage, roll D100 to see if the commander accepts the recruit in his company. Experienced soldiers have a chance equal to 
[container:indent-20]
[br]
(100 - # higher level people in the company / 100) / 3 times (# in the troop / 100)
[br][br]
[/container]
of being offered a command position of some sort (the greater the difference between roll and figure to beat, the better the position should be).

[container:tablebox-60]
 [center] [b]Mercenary Weekly Pay Scales[/b] [/center]

[br]
Pay in a mercenary company is usually in arrears unless the group has just returned from a relatively successful campaign. Normally, the commander is responsible for maintenance of the men (room and board). Weaponry, armor and clothing are the responsibility of the men themselves, as are other than basic daily food requirements (beer, steak, etc.). Those commanders who have developed large companies usually have some in-city guard position or easy caravan duty to provide income for their men when they are not engaged on active duty. 

Should anyone wish to hire a mercenary company or possibly found a troop, the above pay scales may be used. To find the cost for a company or troop, total the amounts for the various types of soldier in the troop by percentage of experience level. The resultant sum is the weekly cost of the mercenary company. Or attempt to hire one of the mercenary bands outlined below.

The normal contract is for a minimum of 4 weeks but this is negotiable, at a cost if the duration is shorter. 

A note to the wise: few mercenary commanders take well to being cheated out of agreed upon sums for duty, whether combat or garrison. It is not unknown for the contractor hiring a company to be overrun and conquered himself when he tried to withhold the mercenaries' hard-earned gold.

[h2]Mercenary Companies In Haven[/h2]
The companies that may be found in Haven at any given time are listed below, including style of fighting (racial type denoted), general type of armor and favored weapon(s), percentages of various experience levels comprising the company, weekly pay requirements (for the entire company), and chance of being in Haven (and therefore available) at any given time. The better companies will usually be less available, since they may get hired as soon as they hit town. 

Note that the amounts in the following tables are what the overall commander of the company receives for use of his men. The men actually receive about one-half to one-third of this money. If the troops are on garrison duty, rather than combat duty, the amounts may be reduced by one-half.

articles.title: Merchants' Guild
articles.content:
Anyone whose focus is on selling (wholesale or retails) and not production. While one might think this a powerful guild it is not the case in Haven. They don't even have their own guildhall and must meet in @[The Hall of All Guilds](landmark:b0995c90-f747-40b6-994b-c052b8b4f278).

articles.title: Monks of Saint Crotofiros
articles.content:
The monks of Saint Crotofiros ("the Bearer of Burdens") operate several street side missions in and around the Labyrinth, providing food and shelter for the homeless children of the area (commonly referred to as "street rats" by those with less reverence). Each meal is served with an obligatory sermon, encouraging the young lads and lasses to keep the faith in their search for a better life. 

Most of the Crotofiran's visitors are already as tough and heartless as the neighborhood that spawned them, and have learned to endure this optimistic poppycock as the price of their free lunch. Once in a while, however, the monks make a convert, who often winds up joining their number permanently, and taking their vows of peace and charity. 

To get the funds necessary to operate their missions, the Crotofirans are little more than beggars themselves, seeking alms daily in the wealthier portions of the city (often giving small seedling plants - "the seeds of tomorrow's growth" - in exchange). The meager cash boxes are never guarded but are seldom robbed, for it is considered highly unlucky to harm or steal from the gentle monks.

articles.title: Ohlfaardt Family
articles.content:
The Ohlfaardts own massive plots of rangeland to the southeast of Haven, and have traditionally been Haven's largest supplier of cured meat products. This role has been somewhat reduced over the years, because 1) the availability of relatively inexpensive magic have made it possible for wealthier merchants to buy storage chests with permanent Cold spells embedded in them; and 2) the expansion of cottage industry in Haven has resulted in the construction of large slaughter-house complexes which are close enough to food markets so that proprietors can pick up a day's worth of fresh meat every morning. 

The Ohlfaardts have unequivocally refused to change in step with the times, and thus have heightened their problems; nonetheless, the sheer size of their herds are such that they continue to make a significant profit from these activities. 

The last five generations of Counts Ohlfaardt have been uniformly miserly men, who enjoyed counting their money more than spending it. The current Count, Neberhard, fit this model perfectly until, at the ripe old age of sixty, he cast caution to the winds, and wed a voluptuous young maiden (@[Sui Tuong Ding](person:908c4354-d81f-42f0-9be5-c227e30d2e2c)) of fifteen while on a journey to exotic @[Herudana](settlement:9165fd2f-d607-4579-83a7-eae6c838997f). The other aristocratic families look upon Neberhard's sudden transformation as scandalous; they consider it even more shocking, however, that he has spent a fortune on fine jewels and gowns to crown his lady's beauty.

The family's home in Haven is located in @[The Heights](settlement:1f0571c0-b8de-4b50-b010-5529cab6603b)

[var:private-access-phre3d]
[/subcontainer]

articles.title: Pavante's Guild
articles.content:
@[Lucius Pavante](person:62476689-79ac-4ec6-af68-4d53ffa38b60), the head of a small law firm in @[The North Corridor](settlement:4554e4a9-db69-47da-9921-6c5850001ce8), is the master of @[The Black Hand](organization:d1a96263-4191-482e-ace6-360d25106f60) guild controlling gambling, loansharking, and protection racketeering. He is a dark headed man of better than average height, who usually dresses well and fancies himself a gourmet. His operation is small, consisting of only some twenty men, but is responsible for far more than its share of total Black Hand revenues. 

One of his men, a mid-level clerk at the @[House of Rand](landmark:a3539989-f5f7-4026-b788-c2e9a20b9c57), serves as the treasurer for Pavante's guild, and maintains an account there that some of the other guilds, particularly @[Angelina](person:b297fe62-c085-4612-a439-fc80b5da899d)'s, draw upon from time to time. Another at the law office, maintains records for loan sharking and protection rackets, which are kept in a locked file in Pavante's personal office. 

Because of Pavante's position as a respected barrister, enforcement against many of his customers is merely a case of utilizing connections with the city guard to bring suit against them. Pavante is able to leak information from his files to proper authorities through a network of well laundered informants, who may well include innocents like players, thus almost ensuring the indictment of those customers who have the temerity to cross him.

articles.title: Public Transit
articles.content:
One of the more interesting features of Haven is its rather unique public transportation system (the uniqueness is in the fact that the city has a public transportation system. Most of the other cities in this region don't). There are some 350 centaurs living in or near the city, and they have organized both a scheduled trip trolley system, and a cab service. 

[subcontainer:1aad823c-af35-4a92-b9c1-93444e9706f4]
The city fathers are so pleased with the centaurs' transportation system that they have arranged free access across the toll bridges for the centaurs and their vehicles - a toll of 5cp  is charged for each passenger, track being kept on a monthly basis, billed to the centaur company. A law has been enacted which classes freeloading on the trolleys as a major infraction, on the same level as theft (an action generally despised in a mercantile society, even though many individual thieves are folk heroes). 

[/subcontainer]
Any person caught by the centaurs attempting to board without prior payment is usually subject to a swift kick with both hind hooves (this will prove not only embarrassing, but painful as well, since the strongest centaurs are assigned to the trolley lines), and a call for the nearest city Guardsman.

[h2]Trolley Service[/h2]
The trolley service runs along three routes from 5:00am to midnight, when the main bridges to @[Trade Island](settlement:eec2fc60-5565-40fa-9047-69924589c400) close. Service is every twenty minutes in both directions and is on time about 70% of the time unless the weather is bad.
[br]
[quote]
[b]The three trolley routes:[/b]
[ol]
[li]From Horse Gate at the south end of Caravan Street to the Caravan Gate at the north end traveling north along South Caravan and south along North Caravan.[br]
[li]a loop through @[The South Corridor](settlement:d684e9e8-9b69-45fc-8352-3de0e6e7f870) and Labyrinth[br]
[li]From the North Gate to the Copper Gate along Gate Street and back
[/ol]
[/quote]

[container:tablebox-40][b]Fares:[/b][br]30cp to ride on one side of the river, and an additional 20cp if one crosses the river. [br]Transfers are not offered, another fare must be paid.[/container] 

The vehicles are large wagons with post-supported canopies, and a set of steps at the front leading to the rows of benches in the bed of the wagon. When not in service, these wagons are stored and maintined at the @[Cab and Trolley Barns](landmark:1fed356b-9b31-44aa-a201-f4a622c1157c) near the @[Horse Market](settlement:3987cb08-4abd-4e55-a582-128df2ff71db).

[h2]Cab Service[/h2]
The cabs are similar in design to the Hansom cabs of Victorian London, and capable of seating up to six in reasonable comfort. They operate on roughly the same time schedule as the trolley and more cabs are on the street from 3:00pm on. 

[container:tablebox-40][b]Fares:[/b][br] 2sp per person per approximate mile (~15 grid squares by road) or part thereof with an additional silver piece to cross a bridge over the Dorian. One can occasionally bargain with the centaur in regard to the price of an entire ride. [/container]

They provide service to all parts of the city proper except the inner Labyrinth, and @[The Dwarrow](settlement:fa91fd8a-f74e-409d-a703-26a9b8e09954). Occasionally They will operate in the suburbs outside the city walls, but the price goes up accordingly; (1-2gp per mile).

[subcontainer:1aad823c-af35-4a92-b9c1-93444e9706f4]
There is a [b]10% chance of a cab within hailing distance from 6:00am to 3:00pm, and a 15% chance thereafter[/b]. After an unsuccessful try one must wait 5 minutes and then whistle again. Centaur cabs are about as easy to locate as a New York taxi on a rainy day.[/subcontainer]

articles.title: Rand Family
articles.content:
The Rands are considered upstarts, having burst upon the Haven financial scene only a decade ago; their flamboyant rise to the near top of the banking pile has made them many enemies, but the three Rand brothers appear unconcerned with others' opinions of them - they are, purely and simply, out to make as much money as they can. 

The head of the group is the eldest brother, @[Kruegger](person:5c9dddff-792e-4a7f-aec6-92497f8d7a97), the most money-wise of the three and under his direction the family's fortunes have increased many fold. The other two, @[Sberri](person:2d08983e-2a01-46eb-b160-6e3d032c0a45) and @[Zalle](person:25c3009e-ebca-4784-a006-a813e43739ee), are good investors, one in land, the other in commodities for export, but neither has the management skill displayed by @[Kruegger](person:5c9dddff-792e-4a7f-aec6-92497f8d7a97). 

The three share an estate in @[The Glens](settlement:e11d38ab-9461-4be2-b745-34ddf86ec7fb) and only @[Zalle](person:25c3009e-ebca-4784-a006-a813e43739ee) is wed, to @[Eezie Rand](person:d9f19bc9-2b69-4601-ae9d-3db42041272f) (nee @[Hofstra](organization:4f2139cf-1498-4093-9f7b-4c3c3f7c8f75), which has prompted rumors of a possible merger between two of the largest fortunes in Haven.

[var:private-access-phre3d]
[/subcontainer]

articles.title: Savonna Family
articles.content:
@[Lady Margery Savonna](person:932609a3-02d8-4138-a3a1-0fab988a932e) is the very wealthy matronly widow of Eklund Savonna, the late lord of Kinselbrock. The family retains substantial feudal land holdings in the west, now governed by Eklund's half-brother, Tolman Burlingame. 

Margery herself has no interest in politics or administration, and much, prefers the active social life of the city. She shares the family's city estate with her younger daughter @[Donia Savonna](person:03cb4f93-d309-48f0-889b-70c1a1d3026e) and a host of servants; her elder daughter, Karys, is a priestess of @[Etheah](person:da6b1939-060c-4f36-8da6-8d4be3b07cf0) at a temple in Kinselbrock.

The @[Savonna Family Residence](landmark:93c45fb7-d64e-46f4-a6ff-6dc284980121) in Haven is located in The @[Heights](settlement:1f0571c0-b8de-4b50-b010-5529cab6603b)

articles.title: Sisters of the Unicorn
articles.content:
Follows the teachings of the goddesses in the Pantheon (except for Laydi), placing their feminity above all.  This does not mean they are against the male gods, or men in general, nor does this impact their sexuality in any way - all women are welcome. 

[b]Symbol:[/b] White unicorn on a black background.
